rulings.Case Title: NATIONWIDE LEGAL INC. VS. WILLIAM D. DOUGLASS
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent William Douglass' Motion For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 10, 2012, Line 8, Respondent William Douglass' Motion For Sanctions Granted. Monetary sanctions of $ 45,719.14 are awarded to Respondent which must be paid by October 31, 2012. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: MADELYN KAHN VS. EUGENE M WOLF MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Eugene Wolf'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set 2 And Request For Production Of Documents, Set 2 (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 10, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT EUGENE WOLF'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set 2 and Request For Production Of Documents, Set 2. (Part 2 of 2.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of tentative ruling.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MADELYN KAHN VS. EUGENE M WOLF MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Two And Further Responses And Docs To Request For Production Of Docs, Set Two
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 10, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT EUGENE WOLF'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set 2 and Request For Production Of Documents, Set 2. (Part 1 of 2.) Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to compel further responses and documents from plaintiff Madelyn Kahn to Dr. Wolf's Special Interrogatories and Request for Production of Documents, Set Two is GRANTED. Plaintiff must produce the requested answers and documents on or before September 17, 2012. (See Part 2.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: EDITH MAZZAFERRI VS. RONALD MAZZAFERRO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Against Defendants; Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 10, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF EDITH MAZZAFERRI Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Against Defendants; Granted. Defendant's underlying anti-SLAPP motion wholly lacked merits and no reasonable lawyer would consider filing it. There is good cause to extend the filing time for this motion such that it is timely filed. Plaintiff is awarded $28,819.11 in fees and costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: C. OVERAA & CO., INC. VS. U.S. GLASS & ALUMINUM, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 10, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT TOFT, DE NEVERS & LEE, CALVIN LEE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion for summary judgment and adjudication is denied. There are triable issues regarding (1) the scope of defendant's work, (2) whether plaintiff was a third party beneficiary of the contract between defendant and U.S. Glass & Aluminum, Inc., and (3) breach and causation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: CONTRACTORS ACCESS PROGRAM OF CALIFORNIA, A VS. MAJESTIC CAPITAL, LTD., A FOREIGN CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Martin Bros./Marcowall, Inc., A California, Mohammad Chahine'S Motion To Compel Bobby Samni/ Asg Law Group Llp To Produce Documents And Appear For Deposition (For Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Line 2, DEFENDANTS MARTIN BROS./MARCOWALL, INC., MOHAMMAD CHAHINE'S Motion To Compel Bobby Samni/ ASG Law Group LLP To Produce Documents And Appear For Deposition. (Part 2 of 2.) The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant and cross-complainant Mohamad Chahine's ("Chahine") motion to compel Bobby Samini and ASG Samini Law Group LLP to produce documents and appear for deposition is GRANTED. Bobby Samini and ASG Samini Law Group LLP shall produce all requested documents within five days of the hearing on this motion. Bobby Samini shall appear for deposition within fifteen days of the hearing on this motion. Bobby Samini shall pay to Chahine within fifteen days of the hearing on this motion sanctions in the sum of $3,720.00, which sum excludes Chahine's travel related fees and costs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (End of tentative ruling.) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LODGING PROPERTY BROKERS, INC. VS. JANE CHANG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Tax Costs And An Order To Compel Judgment Creditor To Acknowledge That Judgment Is Satisfied In Full
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 10, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANT JANE CHANG MOTION And Notice Of Motion To Tax Costs And An Order To Compel Judgment Creditor To Acknowledge That Judgment Is Satisfied In Full Denied. The initial judgment amount was $60,414.75. On July 3, 2012, Defendant filed an amended costs memo for $1,836.40. Those costs became part of the judgment per CRC 3.1700(b)(4). With interest, the amount of the judgment was $63,046.58. Plaintiff's tender on August 6, 2012 was for less than that amount. Accordingly, Defendant's fee motions are timely. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 10, 2012, Line 7, DEFENDANT BOLIGALA RAJU, HIGH QUALITY ORGANICS, INC., A NEVADA CORPORATION MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Off calendar in light of the court's ruling on Defendants' demurrer. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN LINDSETH VS. BOLIGALA RAJU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 10, 2012, Line 7, DEFENDANT BOLIGALA RAJU, HIGH QUALITY ORGANICS, INC., A NEVADA CORPORATION DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action and sustained with fifteen days leave to amend as to the second and third causes of action.. As to the first cause of action, Plaintiff has not pled any prospective controversy that requires declaratory relief. As to the second cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege an injury-in-fact that flowed from the non-compete clause and an unfair business practice that caused Defendants to wrongfully withhold money from Plaintiff. As to the third cause of action, Plaintiff is granted leave to amend, if he can do so in good faith, to allege why that he lacks an adequate legal remedy. Defendants' request for judicial notice is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN LINDSETH VS. BOLIGALA RAJU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Dismiss Or Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 10, 2012, Line 7, DEFENDANTS BOLIGALA RAJU, HIGH QUALITY ORGANICS, INC., A NEVADA CORPORATION Mtn To Dismiss Or Stay Proceedings Denied. The forum selection clause is permissive and Defendants have not shown either that California is a seriously inconvenient forum or that the interests of justice require that this case be in Nevada. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
